questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding GSAT-20 (GSAT-N2) satellite: • It is a communication satellite designed mainly to provide high-speed broadband internet services across India. • The satellite is intended to be placed in a Geostationary Orbit around the Earth. • The satellite will be launched using the GSLV Mk III rocket developed by Indian Space Research Organization. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct because GSAT-20 (GSAT-N2) is a high-throughput communication satellite meant to provide broadband internet services. Statement 2 is correct because it will operate in Geostationary Orbit. Statement 3 is incorrect because the satellite is planned to be launched by SpaceX’s Falcon 9 rocket, not GSLV Mk III.

questions.question: The Rashtrakuta victory over the Cholas under Krishna III is recorded in which inscription?
questions.explanation: The Atakur Inscription is an important historical record from the Rashtrakuta period. It mentions how Krishna III, one of the most powerful Rashtrakuta kings (reigned c. 939–967 CE), defeated Prantaka I of the Chola dynasty as part of his southern campaigns. These campaigns were aimed at extending Rashtrakuta influence over the Tamil region and asserting dominance over the Cholas, Cheras, and other southern kingdoms. The inscription also praises Krishna III’s military achievements and describes the submission of defeated kings, giving historians evidence of the political and military landscape of 10th-century South India

questions.question: Where was the first successful cotton textile mill established in India in 1854?
questions.explanation: The first cotton textile mill in India was set up in Kolkata in 1818, but it was not successful and closed down soon after due to technical and financial problems. The first successful cotton textile mill was established much later, in Mumbai in 1854, known as the Bombay Spinning and Weaving Company.

questions.question: Article 263 of the Indian Constitution deals with:
options: Set up a National Development Council for economic planning among States
Constitute an Inter-State Council to promote coordination between the Union and States
Appoint a single arbitrator for disputes between any two or more States
Form a Finance Commission to recommend sharing of resources between Union and States
questions.explanation: Article 263 of the Indian Constitution allows the President of India to set up an Inter-State Council if it appears necessary for better coordination between the Union and the states, or among the states themselves. Purpose: • To resolve disputes between states or between states and the Union. • To promote cooperation and coordination in policies and administration. • To discuss matters of common interest such as finance, planning, and development. Key points: • The council can be permanent or temporary, depending on the President's decision. • Members usually include the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers of states, and Union ministers. • It is a constitutional body, established by the Constitution. • Its recommendations are not binding but have political and administrative importance.

questions.question: Which of the following Manipuri instrument is made of bamboo, has multiple pipes, and is played by blowing air.
questions.explanation: Theiphit is a traditional wind instrument of the Thadou community in Manipur. It has three bamboo pipes of different lengths, each producing a distinct note when air is blown through them. Pena is a stringed fiddle, Gogona is a Jew’s harp. Pung - A classical Manipuri drum used in Manipuri dance and Sankirtana. It is played while performing the famous Pung Cholom dance.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Inaugural Fiscal Health Index 2025 released by NITI Aayog: • The index evaluates the fiscal health of 18 major Indian states based on five sub-indices. • Odisha, Kerla and Haryana ranked as top three performing states. • Punjab, Andhra Pradesh, and West Bengal were among the states with the lowest fiscal performance. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. Key details about Fiscal Health Index 2025 : The FHI 2025 is the first-ever (inaugural) edition of a comprehensive index created by NITI Aayog to evaluate the fiscal health of 18 major Indian states. The index uses data from the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) for the financial year 2022‑23. The index is built around five major sub‑indices: • Quality of Expenditure • Revenue Mobilisation • Fiscal Prudence • Debt Index • Debt Sustainability These components are combined to create a composite fiscal health score for each state. According to the report: • Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Goa, Jharkhand, and Gujarat topped the index, showing the best fiscal health among all states. • States like Punjab, Andhra Pradesh, and West Bengal were among those with weaker fiscal performance, largely due to higher debt burdens and fiscal deficits.

questions.question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are TRUE? I. Fuse is generally placed in series with the device. II. Fuse is generally placed in parallel with the device. III. Fuse wire has high melting point.
questions.explanation: To avoid the dangers of short circuit fuse wires are placed in different circuits. Fuse wire made up of, tin and lead alloy. Its melting point is low and the resistance is high. It is graded with one of the connective wires or devices of the circuit. When for some reason the value of the current in the circuit exceeds a specified limit, the fuse wire is immediately heated and melts and breaks the circuit thus stopping the flow of current. Hence only statement (I) is correct.

questions.question: Inivalappil Mani Vijayan, who received the Padma Shri in 2025, is associated with which of the following sports?
questions.explanation: Inivalappil Mani Vijayan, popularly known as I. M. Vijayan, is a former Indian footballer and one of the most famous strikers in Indian football history. He received the Padma Shri in 2025 for his contribution to the sport of football.

questions.question: Gogabeel Lake, which was recently recognized as a Ramsar Site, is located in which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Gogabeel Lake is located in the Katihar district of Bihar. It was recently designated as a Ramsar Site due to its ecological importance, especially as a habitat for migratory birds and rich wetland biodiversity.

questions.question: When Ferrous sulphate is heated, then a decomposition reaction takes place. Which of the following products are obtained? I. Ferric Oxide II. Sulphur dioxide III. Sulphur trioxide
questions.explanation: When Ferrous sulphate crystals are heated strongly, they first lose water and then decompose to form Ferric oxide, Sulphur dioxide and Sulphur trioxide. Chemical reaction: 2FeSO4 → Fe2O3 + SO2 + SO3 Therefore, all three products (I, II and III) are formed.

questions.question: Assertion (A): The Chelliah Committee emphasized the need for fiscal consolidation in India. Reason (R): The committee recommended reducing the fiscal deficit to ensure macroeconomic stability.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: The Chelliah Committee did recommend fiscal consolidation and reforms in taxation. While it also recommended reducing the fiscal deficit, the reason given in R is not the direct explanation for the assertion; fiscal consolidation was a broader recommendation including improving revenue collection and controlling expenditure, not solely for macroeconomic stability. The Chelliah Committee, chaired by R. Narasimhan Chelliah in 1991–92, was set up to review India’s tax structure and suggest fiscal reforms. It recommended reducing direct taxes to improve compliance, rationalizing indirect taxes to remove multiplicity of levies, simplifying the tax system, controlling government expenditure for fiscal consolidation, and strengthening tax administration to curb evasion. Its suggestions laid the foundation for major tax reforms in the 1990s, influenced the development of GST later, and helped make the Indian tax system more efficient and growth-oriented in the post-liberalization era

questions.question: Which Delhi Sultan established an employment office to help the unemployed?
questions.explanation: Firoz Shah Tughlaq (1351–1388) introduced several welfare measures during his reign. To help unemployed people find work, he established an employment office. He also created the Diwan-i-Khairat department to provide assistance to the poor, widows, and other needy people. Several important works by Firoz Shah Tughlaq : He built several irrigation canals to improve agriculture. Important canals included: • Yamuna to Hansi canal • Sutlej to Ghaggar canal • Ghaggar to Firozabad canal He founded many new towns and cities, including: • Firozabad (in Delhi) • Hisar-i-Firoza (present-day Hisar, Haryana) • Fatehabad • Jaunpur (developed during his rule) He brought two Ashokan pillars from Topra (Haryana) and Meerut and installed them in Delhi. He built hospitals (Dar-ul-Shifa) and provided free treatment and medicines to people. He abolished several harsh taxes and followed the four Islamic taxes: • Kharaj (land tax) • Khums (war booty tax) • Jizya (tax on non-Muslims) • Zakat (religious charity tax)

questions.question: Who has been appointed as First Woman President of Namibia in March 2025?
questions.explanation: Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah became the first woman President of Namibia after winning the 2024 presidential election. She was officially sworn in on 21 March 2025, marking a historic milestone for the country. Namibia Capital: Windhoek Namibia Currency: Namibian Dollar (NAD)

questions.question: Consider the following statements: • The minimum strength of a State Legislative Council is 40 members • The Governor can dissolve the Council in consultation with the Chief Minister • The Legislative Council can delay non-money bills for a maximum of 4 months. Which of the above statements is/are true?
questions.explanation: The Legislative Council is the upper house in some State legislatures, established under Article 171. It is a permanent body with one-third of members retiring every two years. Its strength cannot exceed one-third of the Legislative Assembly and has a minimum of 40 members (7 in smaller States). Members are elected by the Legislative Assembly, local authorities, graduates, teachers, and nominated by the Governor for expertise in various fields. It can delay ordinary bills up to 4 months but has no power over Money Bills. States can create or abolish a Council via a law approved by Parliament.

questions.question: Which observation best supports the statement that humans have double circulation of blood?
options: Blood passes through the heart twice in one complete circulation
Blood flows only once through the heart in one complete cycle
Right side of the heart pumps oxygenated blood
Oxygenated and deoxygenated blood mix in the heart
questions.explanation: In humans, blood circulation occurs through two separate loops: pulmonary circulation and systemic circulation. In pulmonary circulation, blood moves from the heart to the lungs and back to the heart. In systemic circulation, blood moves from the heart to the rest of the body and returns to the heart again. Thus, blood passes through the heart two times in one complete cycle, which is called double circulation.

questions.question: Which of the following matters does not falls under the Concurrent list of Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Insurance is included in the Union List of the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution. Therefore, only Parliament has the power to make laws on this subject. Distribution of subjects in Seventh Schedule • Union List – 100 subjects • State List – 61 subjects • Concurrent List – 52 subjects Mentioned in Article 246 of the Constitution. Subjects transferred from State List to Concurrent List by the 42nd Amendment (1976) • Education • Forests • Protection of wild animals and birds • Weights and measures • Administration of justice

questions.question: The British government used a secret code for emergency arrangements in case Mahatma Gandhi died during his fast in Aga Khan Palace Jail in 1943. What was the name of this secret code?
questions.explanation: During Mahatma Gandhi’s fast in Aga Khan Palace Jail in 1943, the British government feared widespread unrest if he died in custody. Therefore, they prepared a secret emergency plan called Operation Rubicon to manage the situation.

questions.question: The Jnanpith Award 2025 was conferred on which of the following personalities?
questions.explanation: Vairamuthu is a renowned Tamil poet, lyricist, and writer. He was awarded the 60th Jnanpith Award in 2025 for his outstanding contribution to Tamil literature. Vinod Kumar Shukla- He is a noted Hindi poet, novelist, and short story writer from Chhattisgarh. He received the 59th Jnanpith Award in 2024 and became the first writer from Chhattisgarh to receive this honour. Jagadguru Rambhadracharya- He is a Sanskrit scholar, spiritual leader, and author. He was jointly awarded the 58th Jnanpith Award in 2023 for his contributions to Sanskrit literature with Gulzar (Sampooran Singh Kalra) for his contribution to Urdu literature. Other important facts : Instituted year – 1961 First award year – 1965 First recipient – G. Sankara Kurup (Malyalam) First woman winner – Ashapurna Devi Awarding body – Bharatiya Jnanpith First Hindi Jnanpith Award winner - Sumitranandan Pant (1968) for Chidambara (poetry collection) First English Jnanpith Award winner Amitav Ghosh (2018)

questions.question: In which of the following years was the National Seeds Policy launched?
questions.explanation: The National Seeds Policy was launched by the Government of India in 2002. Its main objective was to ensure the availability of high-quality seeds to farmers, promote the development of the seed industry, encourage private sector participation, and enhance agricultural productivity.

questions.question: Which convention, ratified in 1998, attempts to safeguard the environment and human health by encouraging shared accountability and collaboration in the global trade of pesticides and hazardous chemicals?
questions.explanation: Rotterdam Convention- The Rotterdam Convention was adopted in 1998 and came into force in 2004. It aims to promote shared responsibility and cooperative efforts among countries in the international trade of hazardous chemicals and pesticides. It introduced the Prior Informed Consent (PIC) procedure, which ensures that countries are informed about hazardous chemicals before they are imported. Basel Convention- Adopted in 1989, it regulates the transboundary movement and disposal of hazardous wastes. Paris Agreement- Adopted in 2015 under the UNFCCC, it focuses on combating climate change and limiting global warming. Stockholm Convention- Adopted in 2001, it aims to eliminate or restrict the production and use of persistent organic pollutants (POPs). Therefore, the correct answer is Rotterdam Convention

questions.question: Which of the following is not considered a financial instrument?
questions.explanation: Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) These are financial instruments traded on stock exchanges that track indices, commodities, or baskets of assets. Currency Swap A currency swap is a financial derivative contract in which two parties exchange principal and interest payments in different currencies. Derivatives Derivatives are financial instruments whose value is derived from underlying assets such as stocks, bonds, commodities, or currencies. Land Land is a real or physical asset and does not represent a tradable financial contract or claim. Therefore, it is not considered a financial instrument.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the 57th Senior National Men’s Kho Kho Championship 2025: • The championship was held in Puri, Odisha. • Maharashtra won the men’s title.
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is correct. The 57th Senior National Men’s Kho Kho Championship 2025 was held in Puri, Odisha. Railways won the men’s title by defeating Maharashtra in the final.

questions.question: Match the following : List I (Government Scheme) List II (Launched Year) A) PM Kisan Samman Nidhi 1) 2015 B) Ujjwala Yojana 2) 2016 C) AMRUT 3) 2019 D) SVAMITVA Scheme 4) 2020
questions.explanation: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi – 2019 Provides income support of ₹6000 per year to small and marginal farmers. Ujjwala Yojana – 2016 Provides free LPG connections to women from below poverty line households. AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation) – 2015 Launched to improve urban infrastructure such as water supply and sewerage systems. SVAMITVA Scheme – 2020 Launched to provide property ownership records to people in rural areas using drone mapping.

questions.question: Which of the following options correctly explains why the eastern coastal plains of India are wider and more deltaic than the western coastal plains?
options: Eastern rivers flow over steep gradients
Eastern rivers form large deltas due to gentle slopes
Eastern plains have more tectonic uplift
Eastern plains receive less tidal influence
questions.explanation: The eastern coastal plains of India are wider because the rivers flowing into the Bay of Bengal such as the Ganga River, Godavari River, Krishna River, and Mahanadi River flow over gentle slopes and deposit large amounts of sediments. This leads to the formation of extensive deltas, making the eastern coastal plains broader. In contrast, rivers flowing to the Arabian Sea have steeper gradients and form estuaries instead of large deltas, resulting in narrower western coastal plains

questions.question: BahuMela in _________ is the major festival held at the Kali Temple in the Bahu Fort twice an year.
questions.explanation: Bahu Mela is a famous festival held at the Kali Temple (also known as Bave Wali Mata Temple) located inside Bahu Fort in Jammu. The fair is organized twice a year during Navratri and attracts a large number of devotees

questions.question: During a fuel subsidy program, a distributor is tasked with providing 20 litres of petrol to each registered vehicle owner at a government cost price of ₹90 per litre. However, the distributor uses a rigged pump that dispenses only 16 litres for every 20 litres recorded. The saved petrol is then sold illegally at a 50% markup over the government cost price. The distributor records show he distributed a total of 4000 litres. upon investigation, he is caught and ordered to pay a fine equal to 150% of his total illegal earning from the black market. What is total fine amount he must pay?
questions.explanation: Total petrol distributed by the distributor according to his record=4000 L Petrol actually distributed by him = 4000×16/20 L =3200 L Thus, the quantity of petrol sold by him in black market =(4000 L-3200 L)= 800 L So,his illegal earning from black market = " 800×₹90×150/100=₹1,08,000 Fine must be paid by him is 150% of his illegal earning, i.e. ₹1,08,000×150/100=₹1,62,000

questions.question: A borrows ₹30,000 from X and Y. X lends him a certain sum of money at 12% simple interest per annum while Y lends him the remaining sum of money at 10% simple interest pel annum. If the total interest paid by A at the end of one year is ₹3,300. What is the sum borrowed from X?
questions.explanation: X lends at 12% and Y lends at 10% Overall rate at which A paid his interest to both X and Y =3300/30,000×100=11% By allegation, we get– So, the ratio of the sums borrowed from X and Y is 1:1. Hence, the sum borrowed from X is = 1/2×₹30,000= ₹15,000

questions.question: If A:B=3:4,B:C=8:9 and C:D=15:16 then A:B:C:D is equal to:
questions.explanation: A:B = 3:4 = 6:8 And B:C = 8:9 Thus, A:B:C = 6:8:9 Now, C:D= 15:16 Now we need to equalize the two values of C in above two ratios at LCM of [9,15] = 45 So, A:B:C = (6:8:9) ×5 = 30:40:45 and C:D = (15:16) ×3 = 45:48 Hence, A:B:C:D = 30:40:45:48

questions.question: Which of the following is a mixed recurring decimal?
questions.explanation: A mixed recurring decimal is a number which contains a non-repeating digit or sequence immediately after the decimal point, followed by a repeating sequence. (e.g., 0.4555…, 0.231444…, 3.02333… etc.) Option A is a terminating decimal as it stops at third decimal. Option B is a pure recurring decimal as 45 repeats. Option D is also a pure recurring decimal as 7 repeats. Option C is a mixed recurring decimal in which 8 is non repeating digit and 3 repeats endlessly.

questions.question: Simplify:
Marked price of an article is 140 percent of the value of discount. Marked price is what percent of the selling price?
questions.explanation: Let the value of discount be ₹100. Then, the Marked Price (MP)=₹140 and, Selling price = MP-Discount =₹140-₹100=₹40 Required %=140/40×100%=350%

questions.question: The average of 7 consecutive even numbers is 42. Find the smallest number in the series.
questions.explanation: There are 7 even numbers, so, ((7+1)/2)th = 4th term would be the middle term and equal to the value of average. So, the 4th term is → 42 and 42 (4th term) → 40 (3rd term) → 38 (2nd term) → 36 (1st term). Hence, the smallest number of the series is 36.

questions.question: A shopkeeper initially sells an item for ₹ B, taking a loss of 15%. Later the decides to re-brand the item. He marks up the product by 30% above the cost price but offers a festive discount of 20% on this new marked price. What is the overall percentage profit or loss for the shopkeeper after the discount?
questions.explanation: Let the CP of the item be ₹100, then MP=₹130 Discount given on the new MP is 20%. So, new SP=₹130×80/100=₹104. Surely, there is a profit of (₹104-₹100)=₹4 Hence, profit %=4/100×100%=4%

questions.question: The amount of certain sum at CI becomes ₹8000 in 2 years and ₹8820 in 4 years. Find the rate of interest per annum.
A 60 kg alloy of Bronze contains copper and tin. It currently has 20% Tin. How much pure Tin must be added to the alloy so that the new mixture contains 40% Tin?
questions.explanation: Initially copper was 80% and finally it will become 60% of the alloy, but the actual quantity of copper won’t change, because no extra quantity of copper will be added. Thus, quantity of copper, ⇒ 80% × initial quantity of Bronze = 60% × final quantity of Bronze. ⇒ (Initial quantity of Bronze)/(Final quantity of Bronze) =3/4 ⇒ Final quantity of Bronze = 60 kg × 4/3 = 80 kg. Hence, pure Tin must be added=(80-60) kg = 20 kg

questions.question: A vertical pole that is 3 m tall casts a shadow of 2 m on the ground. At the same time, a nearby cell tower casts a shadow that is 60 m long. what is the height of the cell tower?
questions.explanation: According to similarity of two triangles, ⇒ Height of Pole / Shadow of Pole = Height of cell tower / Shadow of cell tower ⇒ 3/2 = (Height of the cell tower)/60 ⇒ Height of the cell tower = 90 m

questions.question: If tanx+cotx=4 find the value of sin2x.
A rectangular glass window has a length of 8 cm and breadth of 15 cm. What is the length of the diagonal metal brace needed to reinforce the window?
questions.explanation: Length of the Diagonal metal brace: √(8²+15²) cm = √289 cm = 17 cm

questions.question: A right pentagonal prism has a regular pentagon base where each side measures 8 cm and the height of prism is 12 cm. If the area of pentagonal base is approximately 110.11 cm2. find the total surface area of the prism.
questions.explanation: Perimeter of base = 5×8=40 cm. curved surface area = 40×12=480 cm² Total Surface area = Curved Surface Area + 2× Area of base. = 480 + 2×110.11 = 480 + 220.22 = 700.22 cm

questions.question: Two similar triangles have areas in the ratio of . What is the ratio of their corresponding altitudes?

A city planner is designing a triangular park with vertices at the following coordinates P(1,2),Q(4,8) and R(10,5). They want to install a fountain at the centroid of the park. What are the coordinates of the fountain?
questions.explanation: Coordinates of the Centroid (G) of a circle

questions.question: x² + y² + z² = 2(3x - 4y + 5z) – 50, the find the value of (x+y+z)

A smartphone company uses three different suppliers X,Y and Z for its screens. Supplier X provides 40% of the screens, Y provides 40% and Z provides 20%. The probability of a screen being cracked upon delivery is 2% from X,3% from Y and 5% from Z. If a screen is picked at random from the warehouse. What is the probability that it is cracked?
questions.explanation: Let's assume there are 1000 screens to be supplied by X,Y and Z together. X supplies 40% of 1000→400 screens →possible crack 2% =8 screens Y supplies 40% of 1000→400 screens →possible crack 3% =12 screens Z supplies 20% of 1000→200 screens → possible crack 5% = 10 screens Total number of possible cracked screens =(8+12+10)=30 Probability that one picked screen is cracked"=30/1000=0.030

questions.question: Two tangents are drawn from an external point P to a circle, touching at points A and B. If the distance form P to center O is 41 cm, and radius is 9 cm. What is length of tangent PB?
questions.explanation: In ∆OPB it is given that OP = 41 cm and radius, OB = 9 cm. PA and PB are two tangents. (OP²=OB²+PB²) (41)²=(9)²+PB² Hence, the length of tangent PB = 40 cm

questions.question: If 15 workers can build a wall in 12 days. In how many days will 10 workers complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Let the required number of days is x. Now, M₁ D₁ = M₂ D₂ or 15 × 12 = 10 × x ⇒ x = 18 Hence, in 18 days 10 workers will complete the same work.

questions.question: A real juice tetra pack is filled with 36.4 litres of juice. Due to a small puncture, 2/7th of the juice leaks out. How many litres of juice remain in the container?
questions.explanation: Remaining juice in the container =36.4 L×5/7=26 L

questions.question: Two trains, one 450 m long and another 350 m long are moving in opposite direction on parallel tracks. Their speeds are 72 km/hr and 54 km/hr respectively. How long will it take for two trains to completely cross each other?
questions.explanation: Time taken to cross each other completely,

questions.question: In a 1000 m race, A beats B by 100 m and B beats C by 150 m. In the same race, by how many metres does A beat C?
questions.explanation: When A covers 1000 m, B covers 900 m. Ratio of distances covered by them in the same time, A : B=10 : 9 When B covers 1000 m, C covers 850 m. So, the ratio of distances covered by them in the same time, B:C = 20:17 Thus, A:B:C=200:180:153. So, when A covers 1000 m, this implies– 200 units→1000 m or,1 unit→5 m So, 153 units→765 m, i.e. C runs only 765 m. Hence, A beats C by (1000 - 765) m=235 m

questions.question: Which of the following options will have a sum equal to 60?
questions.explanation: Option (C), 35 (2/3) + 24 (1/3)=60. In these types of questions try to add fractions first, if it adds to 1 then proceed with that option.

questions.question: How many 3-digit numbers can be formed from the digits and 9 which are divisible by and none of the digits is repeated?
questions.explanation: For a number to be divisible by 5, the unit digit must be 5 or 0. So, the unit digit, which is 5 in the given scenario, can be selected only in one way from 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9. Since no repetition is allowed, one digit for the hundreds digit place can be selected from remaining 5 digits in five ways. Similarly, one more digit for the thousands digit place can be selected from remaining 4 digits in four ways. Hence, total number of 3-digit numbers which are divisible by 5 is = 1× 5 × 4 = 20.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Having witnessed the catastrophic failure of the previous prototype,
the chief engineer bade the junior technicians not to repeat the same
procedural oversights during the final phase of the high-pressure testing.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) 👉 Replace "not to repeat" with "not repeat" 📌 Error concept: The verb 'bid' (past tense: bade) is a special verb that takes a Bare Infinitive (V1 without 'to') in the Active Voice. Grammatical concept: In English grammar, certain verbs like bid, let, make, see, hear, watch, and help are followed by an object and then a Bare Infinitive (the base form of the verb without "to"). Structure: Subject + bade + Object + V1 (without 'to') Since the sentence provided is in the Active Voice (the chief engineer did the bidding), the "to" before "repeat" must be removed.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. As the marathon entered its final five kilometers, the weaker runners began to __________ from the lead group, unable to maintain the blistering pace.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 📌 Explanation - Drop away means to become fewer in number or to gradually become distant or separate from a group. In the context of a race, when runners cannot keep up with the leaders, the distance between them increases, and they "drop away" from the pack. Drop away: संख्या में धीरे-धीरे कम होना - To decrease in number or amount Example: As the night wore on, the supporters began to drop away, leaving only a few dedicated fans at the stadium. Drop in: बिना सूचना के मिलने आना - To pay an informal or unexpected visit to someone. Example: I thought I’d drop in on my way home to see how you were doing. Drop off: सो जाना / कम हो जाना - To fall asleep, or to decrease in quality/quantity. Example: I was so tired that I dropped off right in front of the television. Drop out: नाम वापस लेना / बीच में ही छोड़ देना - To stop participating in a competition, school, or a society. Example: He had to drop out of the race due to a sudden muscle cramp.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The District Magistrate apprised the citizens about the new safety protocols to be followed during the upcoming festival.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) The sentence requires the correct preposition to follow the verb "apprise." While "about" is commonly used in casual speech to mean "regarding," it is grammatically incorrect when paired with "apprise" in formal competitive exams. The Rule: The verb Apprise (meaning to inform or tell someone) always takes the preposition 'of'. Structure: Subject + apprise + someone + of + something. Hindi Meaning: अवगत कराना / सूचित करना

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: No sooner had the bell rung when the students rushed out of the classrooms.
I have been knowing him for more than a decade, so I can vouch for his integrity.
Having been finished the task, he went to the market to buy some groceries.
Seldom does he visit his hometown nowadays due to his hectic work sched
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct. It uses the rule of Inversion. When a sentence begins with a negative adverb or adverbial phrase like "Seldom," "Never," or "Hardly," the auxiliary verb (does) must come before the subject (he). Detailed Analysis of Other wrong Options: Option A: Error: The conjunction "when" should be replaced with "than." When a sentence begins with "No sooner," it is always followed by the correlative conjunction "than." The conjunction "when" is used only with words like Hardly, Scarcely, or Barely. Option B: Error: It should be "I have known him." The verb "know" is a stative verb (representing a state of mind). Stative verbs are generally not used in the continuous/progressive tense ("have been knowing"). To show an action that started in the past and continues to the present, the Present Perfect Tense should be used. Option C: Error: It should be "Having finished the task." This sentence uses a Perfect Participle. Since the subject ("he") performed the action of finishing the task himself, the active form (Having + V3) is required. The form used in the option ("Having been finished") is passive and implies that the task did something to him, which is logically incorrect.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The manager’s lucid instructions ensured that the team completed the complex project without any confusion or errors.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d). 📌 Explanation - The sentence highlights that the instructions led to completion "without any confusion." This implies the instructions were very clear and easy to understand. The word "lucid" means expressed clearly or easy to understand, which perfectly fits the context. Therefore, no improvement is needed. 📌 Vocabulary & Contextual Analysis Lucid: स्पष्ट / सुबोध - Expressed clearly; easy to understand Example: The professor’s lucid explanation of the law helped the students pass the exam easily. Ambiguous: अस्पष्ट / द्विअर्थी - Open to more than one interpretation; having a double meaning; not clear. Example: The contract was so ambiguous that both companies ended up in court over its interpretation. Intricate: जटिल / पेचीदा - Very complicated or detailed. Example: The artist spent months creating the intricate patterns on the ceiling of the cathedral. Convoluted: घुमावदार / अत्यंत जटिल - Extremely complex and difficult to follow (often used for arguments, sentences, or plots). Example: The politician gave a convoluted excuse for his absence that nobody really believed.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The manager decided to __________ the redundant positions in the department to streamline operations and reduce unnecessary expenditure.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) 📌 Explanation - The sentence discusses "streamlining operations" and "reducing expenditure" by removing "redundant positions" (jobs that are no longer needed). In the context of systems, practices, or formal positions, abolish is the most appropriate term for officially ending or doing away with something. Meaning and Examples of all options: Accentuate: ज़ोर देना / उभारना - To make something more prominent or noticeable. Example: Use a highlighter to accentuate the important points in your notes. Abrogate: (कानून को) रद्द करना - To repeal or do away with a formal law or agreement. Example: The Supreme Court has the power to abrogate laws that are unconstitutional. Abolish: उन्मूलन करना / समाप्त करना - To formally put an end to a system, practice, or institution. Example: Abraham Lincoln worked hard to abolish slavery in the United States. Alleviate: कम करना / राहत देना - To make suffering or a problem less severe. Example: This new medicine will help to alleviate your back pain. 📌NOTE: There is slight differnce between abolish and abrogate. Abolish is used for systems, customs, or specific job positions. Abrogate is used for formal laws, treaties, or constitutional rights.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: The criteria for selecting the most suitable
candidate for the post of Executive Director
was finalized after a long series of deliberations.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 👉 Replace "was finalized" with "were finalized" 📌 Error concept: Subject-Verb Agreement (Number consistency). The verb must agree with the true subject of the sentence, not the nearest noun. Grammatical concept: In this sentence, the subject is "The criteria." Criterion is the singular form. Criteria is the plural form. Since the subject "Criteria" is plural, it requires a plural verb "were" instead of the singular verb "was." Learners often get confused because "criteria" ends in 'a', or they mistakenly look at "Executive Director" (singular) as the subject. Key Rule: When the subject and the verb are separated by a prepositional phrase (in this case, "for selecting the most suitable candidate..."), always ignore the phrase to identify the real subject.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The archer took a deep breath and prepared to bow before the king after winning the tournament.
options: She tied a beautiful silk bow on the gift box to make it look elegant.
The performers had to bow multiple times as the audience cheered loudly.
It is customary to bow your head as a sign of respect in many cultures.
The diplomat refused to bow to the pressure of the opposing party
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) 📌 Explanation- This is a Homonym question. Homonyms are words that have the same spelling and often the same pronunciation but different meanings and grammatical functions. In the Question: "Bow" is used as a Verb which means to bend the knee or body as a sign of respect or greeting (झुककर प्रणाम करना). In Option A: "Bow" is used as a Noun which means a decorative knot made of ribbon (धनुष के आकार की गाँठ/रिबन). Since this uses the word in a completely different grammatical category (Noun) with a different meaning, it is the correct homonym choice. 📌 Why other options are wrong: All other options (B, C, and D) use BOW as a Verb in the sense of bending or yielding, preserving the same core grammatical category and meaning as the highlighted word in the main sentence.

questions.question: Given below are six sentences taken from a paragraph. The first and the last sentences are numbered S1 and S2 and are placed in the first and last positions respectively and are fixed in their positions. The other four sentences between the first and last sentences are jumbled up and are numbered A, B, C, and D. Rearrange the jumbled sentences in a logical sequence keeping the unity and coherence of the paragraph intact. Choose the correct alternative that best describes the sequence. S1. In front of this building, overlooking the river, is a garden, where the once well-trimmed box borders that used to define the walks now grow wild as they list. A. The rich vegetation of those weeds that we call foul adorns the sloping shore. B. The wall fruit grows like hedges against the walls. C. Several willows that spring from the Loire have grown as rapidly as the hedge that encloses it and half conceal the house. D. Fruit trees, neglected for the last ten years, no longer yield their harvest, and their shoots form coppices. S2. Paths once gravelled are overgrown with moss, but to tell the truth, there is no trace of a path.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is (C) - CADB 📌 Explanation - Step-by-Step Logic: Analyze S1 (The Anchor): S1 introduces the setting—a garden in front of a building overlooking a river. It establishes the theme of "neglect" and "wild growth." Find the First Link (C): Sentence C mentions the "Loire" (a river) and how willows from it "half conceal the house." This directly connects to S1's mention of the building and the river. It sets the scene from the outside in. Identify the Mandatory Pair (D and B): Sentence D talks about "Fruit trees" being neglected. Sentence B follows up on this theme by mentioning "wall fruit." Usually, when a passage describes a neglected garden, it moves from general vegetation (willows/weeds) to specific garden features like fruit trees and walls. Connecting to S2: Sentence B mentions "hedges against the walls," and Sentence A mentions "the sloping shore." Finally, S2 concludes by talking about the "paths" that are now invisible. Note how A (weeds on the shore) and B (fruit on walls) describe the chaotic growth before S2 sums it all up by saying you can't even see the paths anymore. Refined Link (C-A-D-B): C connects to the river/house in S1. A adds to the description of the shore/vegetation. D introduces the neglected fruit trees. B adds more detail about the fruit/walls. S2 concludes with the state of the paths. 📌 Final Paragraph (S1) In front of this building, overlooking the river, is a garden, where the once well-trimmed box borders that used to define the walks now grow wild as they list. (C) Several willows that spring from the Loire have grown as rapidly as the hedge that encloses it and half conceal the house. (A) The rich vegetation of those weeds that we call foul adorns the sloping shore. (D) Fruit trees, neglected for the last ten years, no longer yield their harvest, and their shoots form coppices. (B) The wall fruit grows like hedges against the walls. (S2) Paths once gravelled are overgrown with moss, but to tell the truth, there is no trace of a path.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Will the authorities provide the necessary documents for the verification process?
options: Would the necessary documents be provided by the authorities for the verification process?
Will the necessary documents being provided by the authorities for the verification process?
Shall the necessary documents provided by the authorities for the verification process?
Will the necessary documents be provided by the authorities for the verification process?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 📌 Explanation - The given sentence is an Interrogative sentence in the Future Indefinite Tense (Will/Shall + Subject + V1 + Object?) Grammatical Concept: When converting an interrogative sentence in the Future Indefinite tense from Active to Passive voice, the structure follows this rule: Active: Will/Shall + Subject + V1 + Object? Passive: Will/Shall + New Subject (Object of active) + be + V3 (Past Participle) + by + New Object (Subject of active)?

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The teacher said to the students, "Can you finish the assignment before the deadline tomorrow?"
options: The teacher asked the students if they can finish the assignment before the deadline the next day.
The teacher asked the students if they could finish the assignment before the deadline the next day.
The teacher requested the students that could they finish the assignment before the deadline tomorrow
The teacher asked the students whether could they finish the assignment before the deadline the following day.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b) 📌 Explanation - To convert this interrogative sentence into indirect speech, several specific changes must occur in the reporting verb, the modal verb, and the time expressions. Grammatical Concept: Reporting Verb: "Said to" changes to Asked (because it is a question). Conjunction: Since the question starts with a helping verb ("Can"), we use if or whether as a connector. Sentence Structure: The interrogative form (Verb + Subject) must change to Assertive form (Subject + Verb). Tense Change (Modal): Since the reporting verb is in the Past Tense ("said"), the modal "Can" must change to "Could". Pronoun Change: "You" (Second person) changes according to the object "the students," becoming "they." Time Expression: "Tomorrow" changes to "the next day" or "the following day."

questions.question: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will inaugurate the International Research Institute of Ayurveda (IRIA), a flagship project of the State government, at Kalliad in Kannur on Wednesday. Established to present Ayurveda as an ___(1)___ system of medicine to the global community, the first phase of the institute’s construction ___(2)___ on a 300-acre campus. The initial phase, built at a cost of ₹200 crore, includes a 100-bed research hospital, a medicinal plant research centre, a nursery, and a palm-leaf manuscript study centre. The institute aims to scientifically ____(3)____ and globally disseminate the principles and practical application of Ayurveda while preserving traditional knowledge, including rare manuscripts for future generations. It ___(4)___ as a premier international research hub in Ayurveda with more than 40 national and international interdisciplinary institutions having expressed interest in ___(5)___ . In the first phase, priority will be given to research and patient care in cancer and ageing. The Malabar Cancer Centre will collaborate in research activities. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 💢 Explanation - The context mentions that the institute is established to "present Ayurveda to the global community." To make a traditional system acceptable globally in modern times, it must be presented as evidence-based (supported by data/proof). Why others are wrong: (a) ancient: While Ayurveda is ancient, the goal of a Research Institute is to modernize its perception, not just state its age. (b) expensive: This would be a negative trait; a government flagship project wouldn't aim to promote a system as "costly." (c) experimental: This implies it is unproven or in a trial phase, which contradicts the goal of making it a "premier hub."

questions.question: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will inaugurate the International Research Institute of Ayurveda (IRIA), a flagship project of the State government, at Kalliad in Kannur on Wednesday. Established to present Ayurveda as an ___(1)___ system of medicine to the global community, the first phase of the institute’s construction ___(2)___ on a 300-acre campus. The initial phase, built at a cost of ₹200 crore, includes a 100-bed research hospital, a medicinal plant research centre, a nursery, and a palm-leaf manuscript study centre. The institute aims to scientifically ____(3)____ and globally disseminate the principles and practical application of Ayurveda while preserving traditional knowledge, including rare manuscripts for future generations. It ___(4)___ as a premier international research hub in Ayurveda with more than 40 national and international interdisciplinary institutions having expressed interest in ___(5)___ . In the first phase, priority will be given to research and patient care in cancer and ageing. The Malabar Cancer Centre will collaborate in research activities. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) 💢 Explanation - The sentence refers to the "construction" which is the receiver of the action. Since the construction was done by someone else (the government/contractors) and is now finished, we need the Passive Voice in the Present Perfect Tense (has been + V3). Why others are wrong: (a) has completed: This is Active Voice; it would mean the "construction" itself performed an action. (b) completed: This is Simple Past Active; grammatically incorrect here as a standalone verb for the subject "construction." (c) is completed: While passive, "has been completed" is more appropriate for a project that has just reached a milestone ready for inauguration.

questions.question: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will inaugurate the International Research Institute of Ayurveda (IRIA), a flagship project of the State government, at Kalliad in Kannur on Wednesday. Established to present Ayurveda as an ___(1)___ system of medicine to the global community, the first phase of the institute’s construction ___(2)___ on a 300-acre campus. The initial phase, built at a cost of ₹200 crore, includes a 100-bed research hospital, a medicinal plant research centre, a nursery, and a palm-leaf manuscript study centre. The institute aims to scientifically ____(3)____ and globally disseminate the principles and practical application of Ayurveda while preserving traditional knowledge, including rare manuscripts for future generations. It ___(4)___ as a premier international research hub in Ayurveda with more than 40 national and international interdisciplinary institutions having expressed interest in ___(5)___ . In the first phase, priority will be given to research and patient care in cancer and ageing. The Malabar Cancer Centre will collaborate in research activities. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 💢 Explanation - The institute aims to "scientifically validate" Ayurveda. To validate means to prove that something is based on truth or fact. This aligns with the "International Research" nature of the institute. Why others are wrong: (a) challenge: A research institute for Ayurveda wouldn't aim to "disprove" its own subject. (b) ignore: This is logically opposite to the purpose of an institute. (d) replace: You cannot "replace" the principles you are trying to disseminate and preserve.

questions.question: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will inaugurate the International Research Institute of Ayurveda (IRIA), a flagship project of the State government, at Kalliad in Kannur on Wednesday. Established to present Ayurveda as an ___(1)___ system of medicine to the global community, the first phase of the institute’s construction ___(2)___ on a 300-acre campus. The initial phase, built at a cost of ₹200 crore, includes a 100-bed research hospital, a medicinal plant research centre, a nursery, and a palm-leaf manuscript study centre. The institute aims to scientifically ____(3)____ and globally disseminate the principles and practical application of Ayurveda while preserving traditional knowledge, including rare manuscripts for future generations. It ___(4)___ as a premier international research hub in Ayurveda with more than 40 national and international interdisciplinary institutions having expressed interest in ___(5)___ . In the first phase, priority will be given to research and patient care in cancer and ageing. The Malabar Cancer Centre will collaborate in research activities. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) 💢 Explanation - The institute "is envisioned" (Passive Voice). An "envision" is a mental image of the future; the institute is conceived or planned by the stakeholders to be a premier hub. Why others are wrong: (a) envisions: This would mean the building/institute itself is "dreaming" or "planning," which is personification and logically weaker than the passive "is envisioned." (b) envisioned / (c) was envisioned: These are Past Tense. Since the institute is just being inaugurated and its journey is beginning, the Present Passive (is envisioned) is more suitable for its current status and future goal.

questions.question: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will inaugurate the International Research Institute of Ayurveda (IRIA), a flagship project of the State government, at Kalliad in Kannur on Wednesday. Established to present Ayurveda as an ___(1)___ system of medicine to the global community, the first phase of the institute’s construction ___(2)___ on a 300-acre campus. The initial phase, built at a cost of ₹200 crore, includes a 100-bed research hospital, a medicinal plant research centre, a nursery, and a palm-leaf manuscript study centre. The institute aims to scientifically ____(3)____ and globally disseminate the principles and practical application of Ayurveda while preserving traditional knowledge, including rare manuscripts for future generations. It ___(4)___ as a premier international research hub in Ayurveda with more than 40 national and international interdisciplinary institutions having expressed interest in ___(5)___ . In the first phase, priority will be given to research and patient care in cancer and ageing. The Malabar Cancer Centre will collaborate in research activities. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) 💢 Explanation - The sentence mentions that more than 40 national and international institutions have "expressed interest in collaboration." In the context of research and academic hubs, institutions work together to share knowledge and resources. The word "collaboration" perfectly fits the idea of multiple entities joining forces for a common goal. Why other options are wrong - (A) competition: While institutions do compete, a "premier international research hub" is designed to bring people together, not to foster rivalry between the 40 interested parties. (C) opposition: This means to disagree or resist. It would be illogical for institutions to express interest in "opposing" a new research flagship project. (D) privatization: This refers to transferring ownership from the government to a private sector. The text mentions it is a "flagship project of the State government," and there is no context suggesting it is being sold to private owners; the interest lies in research partnership.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Treason Let’s see the meaning of all words: Treason - राजद्रोह - the crime of betraying one's country Example: He was accused of treason. infinitesimal - अत्यंत छोटा - extremely small Example: The chances of winning are infinitesimal. banal - नीरस - dull or unoriginal Example: The movie's plot was quite banal. innocuous - हानिरहित - harmless Example: The comment was meant to be innocuous.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The software supports various truncates for different file types.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Prune Let’s see the meaning of all words: Accrue - जमा होना - to accumulate or gather Example: Benefits will accrue to the company over time. Prune - काटना - to trim or cut Example: She pruned the bushes in her garden. Imbibe - अवशोषित करना - to absorb or take in Example: Plants imbibe water through their roots. Brusque - कठोर - abrupt or rough in manner Example: His brusque reply surprised everyone.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Copious
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Sparse Let’s see the meaning of all words: Copious - प्रचुर, भरपूर - abundant - having a large quantity Example: The artist was praised for his copious work. Cede - सौंपना - to give up or transfer Example: The country ceded the territory to its neighbor. Fomenter - उकसाने वाला - one who instigates Example: He was accused of being a fomenter of trouble. Flee - भागना - to run away Example: The thief tried to flee the scene. Sparse - विरल, कम - scarce or not abundant Example: The desert has sparse vegetation.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. For keeps
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Forever Let’s see the meaning of idiom; For keeps - हमेशा के लिए - forever - for a permanent or indefinite period Example: He left the town for keeps.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Seize
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Grasp Let’s see the meaning of all words: Seize - पकड़ना, जब्त करना - to take hold of something suddenly or forcefully Example: The police seized the smuggled goods. Tyro - नवसिखिया - a beginner or novice Example: He's a tyro in the field of photography. Fidgety - बेचैन - restless or unable to stay still Example: The child was fidgety during the long lecture. Grasp - समझना, पकड़ना - to take hold of something firmly Example: He grasped my hand tightly. Trim - सजाना, काटना - to make something neat or tidy Example: She trimmed the edges of the paper.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The parched landscape was a stark reminder of the need for conservation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Scorched Let’s see the meaning of all words: Parched - सूखा, शुष्क - extremely dry or thirsty Example: The parched earth needed rain. Scorched - झुलसा हुआ - extremely hot and dry Example: The scorched earth after the fire. Boggle - हैरान करना - to confuse or astonish Example: The news boggled my mind. Shrewd - चतुर - clever and intelligent Example: He's a shrewd businessman. Contend - मुकाबला करना - to compete or struggle Example: The team will contend for the championship.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Of primary importance
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Basal Let’s see the meaning of all words: Infant - शिशु - a very young child Example: The infant was sleeping peacefully. Basal - मूलभूत, प्राथमिक - of or forming the base or starting point; fundamental Example: The basal metabolic rate is the rate at which the body uses energy. Entice - ललचाना - to attract or tempt someone Example: The shop entices customers with discounts. Corpse - शव - a dead body Example: The police found a corpse in the morgue.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The investigation found he had clean hands in the scandal.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Innocent Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Clean hands - निर्दोष, पवित्र हाथ - Innocent or guilt-free, without any wrongdoing or involvement in something bad Example: After the audit, the accountant emerged with clean hands, proving he hadn't embezzled any funds.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A person who hates marriage
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Misogamist Let’s see the meaning of all words: Hutch - पिंजड़ा - a small cage or enclosure for animals Example: The rabbit hutch needed cleaning. Lair - गुफा - a secret or hidden place, especially a den for wild animals Example: The tiger retreated to its lair. Kiln - भट्ठा - a furnace or oven for burning, baking, or drying things Example: The pottery was fired in a kiln. Misogamist - विवाह-द्वेषी - a person who hates marriage Example: He's a confirmed misogamist, preferring his freedom.

questions.question: There are 11 members in a family. Anisha and Amit are sister and brother respectively whose father’s sister’s father is Abhinav, who is husband of Arzoo. Akash and Ajay are brothers of Anamika and Anju is wife of Akash, whose son is Amit. Aman is the brother of the grandmother of Anisha. Aisha is the mother of Anjali, whose father is Aman. How is Anju related to Arzoo?
questions.explanation: Anju is daughter in-law of Arzoo.

questions.question: If ‘CAT’ is coded as ‘D1V’, how is FUN coded?
questions.explanation: VOWELS are coded as 1.

questions.question: If ‘NOON’ is written as ‘OPPM’, how is ‘RATS’ written?

Study the following information and answer the given question: A % B means A is husband of B. A # B means A is father of B. A $ B means A is sister of B. Then which of the following relations is true to imply that K is paternal aunt of V?
Which of the following letter will come next to the series? Y, Q, K, G, E, ?
What will be the 4th term in the following series? 16, 64, 256, ?
Find out the wrong number in the following series? 1726, 1329, 998, 731, 510, 341
questions.explanation: 1726 = 12³ - 2 1329 = 11³ - 2 998 = 10³ - 2 731 = 9³ + 2 (WRONG) 510 = 8³ - 2 341 = 7³ - 2 ⇒ 731 is the odd one out of the listed numbers.

questions.question: B is to E as R is to ?
questions.explanation: ⇒ E is the First vowel in alphabetical series when you start from B, Similarly U is the first vowel in alphabetical series when you start from R.

questions.question: What is the position of the word ‘PLACID’ when the following words are arranged alphabetically? 1. PLACED 2. PLACID 3. PLACEMENT 4. PLEDGE 5. PLACING
questions.explanation: Correct order is as follows. 1. PLACED 2. PLACEMENT 3. PLACID 4. PLACING 5. PLEDGE

questions.question: Find the missing letter:
Find the odd one out in the given Semantic Series based on pattern identified. Violet, Blue, Yellow, Purple
questions.explanation: Violet, blue and yellow are part of visible right Spectrum or Rainbow colours (VIBGYOR), whereas purple is not part of that spectrum.

questions.question: Find the odd number pair:
questions.explanation: 3×2=6 2×1=2 3×4=12≠10 9×1=9 Hence, option 3 is different.

questions.question: In the following figure, what will come in plate of question mark?
After interchanging + & × and 6 & 18, which of the following equation will holds true?

5 people P, Q, R, S, T are sitting around a circular table facing towards the centre (not necessarily in the same order) Q is sitting Second to the right of S. P is sitting second to the right of Q. R is not an immediate neighbour of P. Who is sitting third to the right of R?

Instruction : Chose the inference(S) what are clearly Supported by the statement. Statement : All employees who are promoted to senior management have completed an MBA and have at least ten years of experience. Inferences : I. An employee who has completed an MBA and has at least 10 years of work experience. II. Having 10 years of work experience alone is not sufficient for promotion to senior management.
options: Neither inference I nor II follow
questions.explanation: Inference I → This does not follows. The statement never says that everyone with these qualifications will be promoted. There could be other criteria (performance, vacancies, leadership skills etc.) Inference II → This follows Since promotion also requires on MBA, experience alone clearly isn’t enough. Hence, only inference II follows

questions.question: You notice a colleague not following the prescribed safety procedure while operating equipment at the workplace. What should you do?
options: Ignore the situation unless an accident actually happens.
Confront them angrily go they stop immediately.
Politely remind them of the correct safety procedure.
Record a video and report it on social media.
questions.explanation: Option C is the best that you can do since it includes immediate correction, polite behavior and focus on safety. This helps prevent accidents while maintaining workplace harmony.

questions.question: Which code violates the pattern: uppercase - Lowercase – uppercase?
Which rows elements are all prime numbers? Row I 3 5 7 9 Row II 2 5 7 3 Row III 2 3 1 8
questions.explanation: Row II contains 2, 3, 5 and 7. All of them are prime numbers

questions.question: In a group of Students, 12 learn Hindi, 8 learn English and 5 learn both. How many learn only English?
questions.explanation: Subtract the number of students who learn both languages from the total number of English learners. Here, Total English learners: 8 Students who learn both languages - 5 Students who learn only English: 8 - 5 = 3

questions.question: B – 8 , C – 27, E – 125, H – 343, ?
questions.explanation: ⇒ alphabets follow +1, +2, +3, +4 pattern while numbers cube of successive prime numbers.

questions.question: Which of the following option best replaces the question mark (?) in the given question:
